title: 10 best herbal essential oils for gastroparesis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for gastroparesis are a natural and effective way to manage symptoms associated with the condition. Gastroparesis is a disorder characterized by delayed gastric emptying, leading to digestive discomfort, bloating, and nausea. Herbal essential oils, such as ginger, peppermint, and chamomile, can help alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation, soothing the digestive tract, and calming the stomach. For example, inhaling ginger oil can help ease nausea and vomiting, while peppermint oil can relax the muscles in the digestive tract, improving digestion and relieving bloating. By incorporating these oils into daily routines through aromatherapy or topical application, individuals with gastroparesis can experience improved quality of life, reduced symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its unique composition, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The oil's properties help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms. Additionally, the oil's ability to stimulate digestion and improve gastric motility can help to alleviate nausea, vomiting, and abdominal discomfort associated with the condition. Overall, maidenhair tree essential oils may provide natural relief for individuals suffering from gastroparesis.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with Gastroparesis, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced discomfort.

Carminative: The carminative property helps to ease gas, bloating, and flatulence, which are common symptoms of Gastroparesis, by stimulating the release of gases from the digestive tract and reducing pressure on the stomach and intestines.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to Gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving symptoms of gastroparesis such as abdominal pain and discomfort.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from healthy trees and rinse them thoroughly under cool running water.
Blend one cup of chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with two cups of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate the infusion process.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil in a cool dark place for at least two weeks before using it topically or aromatically.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its natural ability to soothe and relax the muscles in the stomach. The menthol content in peppermint oil can help to calm down the spasms and cramps associated with gastroparesis, allowing for easier digestion and reduced nausea. Additionally, peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, peppermint essential oils may provide relief from gastroparesis-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint oil's digestive stimulant property helps to improve digestion, relieve bloating, and reduce nausea, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of peppermint oil help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps, spasms, and pain associated with gastroparesis.

Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are used because they contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.

Barks: The barks of peppermint are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier in the early morning.
Rinse the harvested peppermint leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steam distill 100g of peppermint leaves with 200ml of distilled water for 30 minutes to release the essential oil.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation or filtering through a coffee filter.
Transfer the collected essential oil to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place away from direct sunlight for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with gastroparesis because its natural properties stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing inflammation and improving gut motility. The oil's bioactive compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to increase the secretion of digestive juices and relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, allowing food to pass more efficiently through the digestive system. This can help alleviate symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain commonly associated with gastroparesis, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those affected.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Herbal ginger essential oil helps stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea, which can be beneficial for individuals with gastroparesis, as it can help improve gastric motility and reduce symptoms of delayed stomach emptying.

Antioxidant: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is common in gastroparesis patients, and may also help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage.

Anti-inflammatory: Herbal ginger essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as abdominal pain, bloating, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good for making medicinal essential oils because they contain active compounds that help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with gastroparesis.
Roots: Roots are often used to create essential oils that can help to soothe digestive issues, including cramping and bloating common in gastroparesis patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale and wash them gently with clean water.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or until they are brittle.
Grind the dried rhizomes into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the ground zingiber officinale powder with 2 parts of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 14 days to allow the essential oils to infuse.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with gastroparesis because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, soothing any inflammation or discomfort. Lavender's natural relaxant properties also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of gastroparesis symptoms such as bloating and nausea. Additionally, lavender oil has antimicrobial properties that can aid in digestion by eliminating harmful bacteria and promoting a healthy gut environment, allowing for proper nutrient absorption and alleviating symptoms associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: English Lavender essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax and calm the stomach muscles to alleviate symptoms such as abdominal cramps, bloating, and nausea associated with Gastroparesis.

Anti-inflammatory: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a common complication of Gastroparesis, and alleviate symptoms like pain and discomfort.

Sedative and Anxiolytic: English Lavender essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for Gastroparesis flares, and promote relaxation and sleep, which are often disrupted in individuals with the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are often used to create essential oils that help alleviate digestive issues, including gastroparesis, due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried l avandula angustifolia flowers in a ratio of 10 parts by weight to one part of carrier oil.
Steam distill the mixture using a clevenger apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store the finished lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
Use 5-7 drops of the essential oil blended with 1 teaspoon of carrier oil applied topically to the stomach area twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with gastroparesis because it has natural digestive properties that can help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea, bloating, and cramps. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can also soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy digestion. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties can help to relieve gas and bloating, allowing food to move more efficiently through the stomach and intestines, thereby providing relief from gastroparesis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel essential oil's carminative properties can help to alleviate bloating, gas, and discomfort in individuals with gastroparesis by reducing the formation of intestinal gas and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.

Digestive stimulant properties: Fennel essential oil has been shown to stimulate digestion and improve the movement of food through the digestive system, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with gastroparesis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which is often a contributing factor to gastroparesis symptoms, and may also help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of essential oil, which has been shown to help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis by relaxing the stomach muscles and improving digestion.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them effective in reducing nausea and vomiting associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare seeds and flowers on a sunny day.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a still or essential oil extractor.
Collect the essential oil produced during the distillation process.
Filter the essential oil through paper towels or coffee filters before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits. The oil's main component, eugenol, has been shown to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramping that can exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms. Additionally, cloves essential oil has natural antibacterial properties that may help combat Helicobacter pylori infections, which are often linked to gastroparesis. By incorporating cloves essential oil into a daily routine, individuals with gastroparesis may experience improved digestion, reduced discomfort, and enhanced overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Clove essential oil can relax and calm spasms in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as abdominal pain and nausea.

Carminative property: Clove essential oil has a natural ability to eliminate gas and reduce bloating, which can be beneficial for individuals with gastroparesis who often experience stomach discomfort and distension.

Anti-inflammatory property: Clove essential oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the clove plant have a strong analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect, making them useful for soothing digestive discomfort associated with gastroparesis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the clove plant contain compounds that help to relax smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh syzygium aromaticum leaves in the early morning when dew drops still present on the surface.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with distilled water to remove any impurities and debris.
Steam distill the fresh syzygium aromaticum leaves using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth into dark glass bottles.
Store the freshly extracted syzygium aromaticum essential oil in a cool, dry place protected from light and heat for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cardamom essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its natural ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and abdominal pain. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is common in individuals with gastroparesis. Additionally, cardamom has been shown to increase gastric motility, allowing food to move more efficiently through the stomach and intestines, reducing symptoms of slow digestion and gut congestion. By promoting a healthy gut environment, cardamom essential oils can provide relief from gastroparesis-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Cardamom essential oil helps to reduce gas and bloating, which is a common symptom of gastroparesis, by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and preventing gas from building up.

Anti-inflammatory property: Cardamom essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and small intestine, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as abdominal pain and discomfort.

Spasmolytic property: The spasmolytic action of cardamom essential oil helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cardamom roots have been traditionally used to ease digestive issues, making them an excellent choice for creating essential oils that can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.

Buds: The buds of the cardamom plant are rich in aromatic compounds and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe stomach discomfort and reduce inflammation associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh elettaria cardamomum pods in the morning after dew has evaporated to ensure highest oil content.
Dry the harvested pods in a warm shade or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture.
Use a steam distillation apparatus with 100-150g of dried elettaria cardamomum pods and 1l of distilled water to extract oil.
Distill the mixture for 2-3 hours or until the oil collection reaches 20ml, monitoring temperature and pressure closely throughout process.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The curcumin in turmeric oil reduces inflammation in the stomach and small intestine, which can alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with gastroparesis. Additionally, turmeric oil's carminative properties help to ease digestion by stimulating the release of digestive enzymes and reducing bloating and gas. As a result, turmeric essential oils may help to improve gastric motility and reduce the severity of gastroparesis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can ease abdominal pain and discomfort associated with gastroparesis.

Digestive stimulant property: The essential oil's ability to stimulate digestion can help improve gastric motility, reducing symptoms of gastroparesis such as delayed emptying of food from the stomach.

Antispasmodic property: Turmeric essential oil's antispasmodic effect can help relax and reduce spasms in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms like abdominal cramping, nausea, and vomiting often experienced by individuals with gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric's rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that can help alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, making them an excellent choice for medicinal essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality c. longa turmeric roots and wash them gently with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Infuse 250g of the turmeric powder with 1l of high-proof neutral carrier oil in a double boiler setup for 2 weeks, shaking daily.
Filter the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its calming properties that can soothe the digestive tract and ease stomach spasms. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may also reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to relax the muscles of the digestive tract, allowing food to move more efficiently through the system and reducing feelings of bloating and discomfort associated with gastroparesis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile oil reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as nausea and stomach pain.
Antispasmodic property: Chamomile oil relaxes the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, helping to reduce spasms and cramps that can occur with gastroparesis.
Sedative property: Chamomile oil has a calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate gastroparesis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are valued for their calming and soothing properties, which can help ease digestive discomfort associated with gastroparesis.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant are rich in apigenin and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and relaxant effects, making them effective for soothing digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or late evening for optimal oil content retention.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 3-5 days, ensuring airflow and minimal handling.
Crush the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into small pieces using mortar and pestle to increase surface area exposure.
Steam distill the crushed matricaria chamomilla mixture at 100°c for 1 hour, collecting essential oil in a clean glass container.
Filter the collected essential oil through coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities or debris, resulting in high-quality chamomile oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with gastroparesis because of its natural properties that aid in digestion. The oil's carvacrol and thymol compounds have been shown to relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and improving motility. This can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain commonly associated with gastroparesis. Additionally, thyme essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce inflammation in the gut, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Thyme essential oil helps to relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with gastroparesis.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme essential oil has potent antimicrobial activity, which can help to combat infections that may contribute to gastroparesis symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and bloating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis such as abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues associated with gastroparesis.
Seeds: Containing a high amount of thymol, a natural antispasmodic agent that can help alleviate abdominal pain and cramps commonly experienced by individuals with gastroparesis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from organic plants with at least 2% thymol content in the morning.
Dry the thyme leaves using air circulation or low heat to preserve thymol and prevent spoilage within 24 hours.
Steam-distill dried thyme leaves in a copper alembic still with a ratio of 1 part thyme to 3 parts water at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Cool the distillation apparatus and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation or filtration within an hour.
Bottle the thymus vulgaris essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool place for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gastroparesis with herbal essential oils is by combining peppermint, ginger, and fennel oil. Peppermint oil helps stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea, while ginger oil reduces inflammation and soothes the stomach lining. Fennel oil relaxes the muscles and eases bloating. Add a few drops of these oils to warm water or use a diffuser for effective relief from gastroparesis symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastroparesis that are treated with herbal essential oils are Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS), Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD), and Dyspepsia. Essential oils such as peppermint, ginger, and fennel have been shown to alleviate symptoms like bloating, abdominal pain, and digestive discomfort associated with these conditions. These oils can also help reduce inflammation, soothe the digestive tract, and regulate bowel movements, providing relief from chronic gut issues.
